title: ferray-strings: uses owned String per element — no fixed-width or interned storage
description:
Question: 

Context: 

Findings: 

NumPy stores strings as fixed-width unicode (dtype U10 = 10 codepoints per element, zero-padded). This means all strings in an array occupy the same space, enabling contiguous memory layout and SIMD. ferray-strings uses Vec<String> where each element is a heap-allocated, variable-length String. This has higher memory overhead (24 bytes per String + heap allocation) and poor cache locality for large arrays. Research: (1) a fixed-width StringArray where each element is [u8; N], (2) a compact string pool (all strings contiguous in one buffer, with offset/length indices), (3) Apache Arrow's StringArray layout (offsets + values buffer).
